originalTitle: South African TV personality who planned to take his life dies aged 61
title: A Sad Goodbye to a Star: Remembering Ian von Memerty
content:
A Bright Star is Gone

Hello everyone! We have some very sad news to share today. A kind and famous person from South Africa has passed away. His name was Ian von Memerty. He was 61 years old. Ian was very well known for making people smile. He was a bright star on our television screens. He was very talented. Many people loved watching him. It is a sad time for his family and friends. We will miss his happy face. We remember him for all the joy he shared. He made a big difference in the world of TV and performing. We want to tell you all about this special person.

Who Was Ian von Memerty?

Ian von Memerty was a super talented person. He was what we call a "TV personality." This means he was on TV a lot! He was known across South Africa. He was a dancer, a singer, and even a judge on many TV shows. Imagine being able to do all of that! He worked very hard to bring fun and laughter to our homes. Ian was also a writer. He wrote many wonderful stories and shows. He was very creative. People loved his shows because they were full of energy. He made everyone feel happy when they watched him. It is sad to say goodbye to someone so full of life. He was a true performer. He brought so much light to people’s lives. **Did you know?** South Africa is a big country in Africa! It has many beautiful places and friendly people. It is also home to many talented artists, just like Ian!

Dancing, Singing, and Smiling

Ian loved to dance. He was a fantastic dancer! He could move his feet so fast. Dancing is a fun way to express yourself. It keeps you fit too! Ian also loved to sing. He had a lovely voice. He performed in many big shows. He would sing songs that made people tap their feet. He was also a wonderful storyteller. He would tell jokes and make everyone laugh. He made everyone smile. He was a judge on TV shows like "Strictly Come Dancing." On these shows, he would watch other dancers. Then he would give them advice. He always had kind words. He helped many people on their journey. He helped them become better dancers. He always cheered them on. He was truly a mentor. **Did you know?** "Strictly Come Dancing" is a TV show where famous people learn to dance with professional dancers! It's super exciting to watch! He was very busy. He did so many different things. He was a musician, which means he played instruments. He was an actor, which means he pretended to be different people in plays and shows. He was a director, which means he helped put shows together. He had many skills. He was always trying new things. He inspired many young people. He showed them that it's good to follow your dreams. He showed them that if you work hard, you can achieve amazing things. He brought so much happiness to homes across our country. His performances were always full of life and colour. We will always remember his bright smile.

What is a TV Personality?

Let's talk about what a "TV personality" is. It's someone who you see a lot on television. They might host shows, or act in them, or be a judge. They become like friends that you welcome into your home. They make you laugh, or teach you things, or entertain you. Ian was one of these special people. He made TV fun for everyone. He shared his talents with millions of people. Think of your favourite cartoon character or the person who reads the news. They are all TV personalities! They help make TV exciting and interesting. Ian von Memerty was one of the best. He knew how to connect with people. He made everyone feel special. He used his platform to spread positivity. **Did you know?** The first television was invented a very long time ago, in the 1920s! Now, almost everyone has a TV in their home. He would often use his shows to talk about important things. He would always try to make people feel better. He was a kind soul. He always had a good word for everyone. He truly cared about his audience. He loved to share his passion for music and dance. He wanted everyone to feel the joy that he felt when he performed. He was a shining example of what a good performer can be. He taught us to embrace our talents. He taught us to share our gifts with the world. He believed in the power of art to bring people together. He made the world a brighter place with his work.

Remembering Our Stars

It is sad when someone special like Ian passes away. But we can remember them in our hearts. We can talk about the good things they did. We can watch old videos of their shows. This helps us to keep their memory alive. Ian had been unwell for some time. He was very brave. Even when he was sick, he shared his stories. He talked about his life and his experiences. He wanted to share his journey with his fans. It showed how much he cared for everyone. When someone famous leaves us, it reminds us to cherish the people we love. It reminds us to be kind to each other. We should always remember the joy they brought. **Did you know?** Many famous people write books or make documentaries about their lives to share their journey with the world! Ian was one of those people. His family and friends will miss him very much. We send our love to them. It is okay to feel sad when someone we admire is gone. But we can also celebrate their life. We can celebrate all the wonderful things they did. Ian von Memerty lived a life full of music, dance, and laughter. He was a true inspiration. He touched many hearts across South Africa and beyond. His legacy of joy and creativity will continue. We will always remember his amazing performances. We will remember his warm smile. He will always be a star in our memories.

Sharing Joy and Kindness

Ian showed us how important it is to share joy. He made people happy with his performances. He made them feel good. He also showed us the power of kindness. He was always encouraging others. He always had a smile to give. Even when things were tough, he found a way to share light. We can all learn from him. We can all try to be a little bit more like Ian. We can share our smiles. We can be kind to our friends and family. We can try to make someone's day better. This is a wonderful way to remember him. His spirit will live on through the happiness he created. Let's promise to keep sharing joy, just like Ian did. Thank you for listening to this important news. It's sad, but it also helps us remember what a wonderful person Ian von Memerty was. Let's keep his memory bright by being kind and sharing our own special talents. Goodbye for now, and remember to always shine!
summary: We share the sad news that Ian von Memerty, a beloved South African TV personality, dancer, and singer, has passed away at 61, and we remember all the joy and kindness he shared with everyone.
whyMatters: It matters because Ian von Memerty brought so much happiness to many people in South Africa with his dancing, singing, and smiling on TV, and we can all learn from his kindness and by remembering to share joy with others.

originalTitle: US sanctions Rwandan army and top officials for supporting M23 in DRC
title: America Asks Rwanda to Help Keep Peace in Congo!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi friends! It's your friendly news reporter here, bringing you important news from our amazing continent, Africa! We live in a big world with many countries. Today, we're going to talk about two special countries that are neighbors: Rwanda and the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DRC for short. They are right next to each other on the map, just like your house might be next to your friend's house. Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the world, with many wonderful countries and people!

Meet Our Neighbors: Rwanda and Congo!

Rwanda is a beautiful country. It's known for its rolling hills and friendly people. The Democratic Republic of Congo, or DRC, is also a very big and beautiful country. It has big forests, important rivers, and lots of different animals. Both countries want to live in peace, where everyone is happy and safe. Did you know? Rwanda is sometimes called the "Land of a Thousand Hills" because it has so many hills and mountains! The DRC is one of the biggest countries in all of Africa!

What is Happening in Congo?

Sometimes, even when we want peace, there can be some trouble. In the DRC, there is a group of people called M23. They are a group of fighters. They have been fighting in some parts of the DRC. When people fight, it can make others sad and scared. It makes it hard for kids to go to school and for families to live safely in their homes. Everyone wishes for the fighting to stop so that peace can return to the country. Did you know? The M23 group got its name from a peace agreement signed on March 23rd! But now, they are fighting again, which makes many people sad.

Helping Our Neighbors

There are many countries in the world. Some big countries try to help other countries when there is trouble. One very big country far away is called America. America cares about peace for everyone, everywhere. They watch what is happening around the world and try to help when they see problems. Did you know? America is very far from Africa, across a big ocean! But they still care about what happens here.

What Did America Do?

America saw that there was fighting in the DRC because of the M23 group. America thinks that the army from Rwanda, and some important people who lead Rwanda, were helping the M23 group. Helping a group that is fighting makes the fighting worse, not better. So, America decided to do something to try and help. Imagine you are playing with your friends, and someone is not playing by the rules. A grown-up might say, "Time out!" or "You can't play with that toy for a little while." This is a bit like what America did. They decided to put "sanctions" on Rwanda's army and some leaders. What does "sanctions" mean for countries? It means they can't do certain things. Like, they might not be able to get some money or special things from America. It's like being told "no" to some playtime activities for a little while. America hopes that these "sanctions" will make Rwanda stop helping the M23 group. They want Rwanda to help bring peace, not fighting, to their neighbor, the DRC. These rules are put in place to encourage peace. America wants all countries to follow rules that help keep everyone safe and happy. They hope Rwanda will listen and help make things peaceful again. Did you know? When countries have "sanctions," it's often to encourage them to change how they are doing things and follow international rules for peace, so everyone can live together happily.

What Does Rwanda Say?

When America said, "We think you are helping the M23 group, and we are putting sanctions on you," Rwanda felt sad. The important people in Rwanda said, "No, this is not fair!" They said that America's decision was "unjustly" targeting them. This means they think America is blaming them unfairly. They believe they are not helping the M23 group. They want everyone to know that they care about peace too. They said that America's decision was not right and that they are being treated unfairly. Did you know? Kigali is the main city in Rwanda, just like your capital city is the main city in your country. When we talk about what Rwanda says, it's often what the leaders in Kigali say.

What Does Congo Say?

The people and leaders in the DRC were happy about America's decision. The main city in the DRC is called Kinshasa. The leaders in Kinshasa said, "Thank you, America!" They welcomed the sanctions. They think that Rwanda *is* helping the M23 group, and they hope that America's "time out" will make the fighting stop. They want peace in their country very much. They hope that this will help M23 stop fighting and allow everyone to live safely again in their homes and go to school. Did you know? Kinshasa is one of the biggest cities in all of Africa! It's a very busy and exciting place with millions of people.

Why Is Peace Important?

Peace means no fighting. Peace means everyone is safe and happy. When there is peace, kids can go to school, families can grow food, and people can play and work without fear. It's like when everyone in your classroom is friends and helps each other. When there is fighting, it hurts everyone, especially children. That's why countries all over the world try to help each other find peace and stop all the fighting. Did you know? The symbol for peace is often a dove, a beautiful white bird! It reminds us that we all want a peaceful world.

Let's Hope for Peace!

So, America made a big decision to try and help stop the fighting between the M23 group and the DRC. They hope that by putting these rules, or "sanctions," on Rwanda's army and leaders, Rwanda will stop helping the M23 group. Rwanda says it's not fair, and the DRC says "thank you." It's a tricky situation, but everyone, especially children in Rwanda and the DRC, hopes for peace soon. We all wish for our neighbors to live happily and safely together, just like good friends should! We hope all the fighting stops so kids can play and learn every day. Did you know? Many countries work together at a big place called the United Nations to try and solve problems and keep peace around the world, so everyone can be safe and happy!
summary: America wants Rwanda to stop helping a fighting group in Congo by setting some rules, but Rwanda thinks it's unfair, and Congo is happy about it.
whyMatters: It matters because everyone wants peace for our neighbors in Rwanda and Congo. When there is fighting, children can't go to school or play safely, so America hopes its rules will help bring peace back to the families in Congo.
